Is your Google better than your competitors?

Right now a homeowner is sizing you up against the shop next to you on Google. Lose that one, and it’s a twenty to thirty thousand dollar kitchen walking out the door before you ever knew it was up for grabs.

Type your business name and I’ll show you exactly where you stand.

Claim your free strategy session →

Updated June 13, 2026

Dear kitchen & bath owner,

Building a business is hard, and you built this one on your work.

Word of mouth, repeat customers, shouting your name from the rooftops.

For years the work brought the work.

Then it slowed. The calendar that filled itself started showing gaps.

One morning you caught yourself wondering where the next job was coming from.

So you tried to fix it.

Maybe Angi sold you tire-kicker leads, Houzz locked you in for a year, or an SEO company took $1,500 a month and mailed you charts.

None of it filled the calendar.

But the customers never left.

The NKBA counts more than 10 million kitchens remodeled in America every year. One in ten households.

The only question is whether they find you, and whether they pick you when they do.

Usually they find you fine. Then they see a tired version of you, parked next to a competitor who looks like the safer bet.

So they call him.

He isn’t better than you. He’s easier to say yes to.

And 92% of homeowners won’t consider a shop under four stars, no matter how good the work is.

So this isn’t you losing your touch.

The homeowner just moved the first handshake to a screen. That’s the one room you’re not standing in yet.

I get you in front of the homeowners already looking, and I make you the obvious call.

I fix what she sees the second she looks you up, build the site that closes her, and keep it working every month.

Get you in the room. Make you the one she calls.

Schedule your free strategy session → 30 minutes, no pitch-slap. You leave with your plan either way.
2.7× more likely to be trusted with a complete Google profile
270% more likely to get picked once you have your first five reviews

— Shane Nguyen, Kitchen Websites

What exactly I do to sell you more kitchens

Get found

SEO + Google

I rebuild your Google, work it every month, and turn every finished job into a review.

Read more →

Get chosen

Converting website

I build the site that turns her click into a booked call.

Read more →

If your calls don’t go up in ninety days, we work for free.

Read that again.

Day one, we write down your numbers. How many calls, how many homeowners find you.

Then I go to work. You watch every week of it.

Ninety days in, more homeowners are calling you than the day we started. If they’re not, you stop paying and I keep working.

Same hours, nothing on the invoice, until that number moves.

Still flat three months later? We shake hands and you keep all of it. The profile, the reviews, the work. Yours.

Picture the calendar ninety days out. The gaps filled. The phone going off while you’re on a job, because they find you first now, not the guy three streets over.

And in ninety days, this offer will be a number you can deposit.

If it isn’t, I haven’t earned a dollar. And I don’t stop until it is.

Here’s how it works.

In thirty minutes you’ll have the exact plan to win more of the jobs you’re losing online right now.

I’ll go through everything a homeowner sees when they’re deciding between you and your three closest competitors, and show you where they’re picking the other guy, and what to fix first so that stops.

You walk away with it in writing. Run it yourself or hand it to me. Either way it’s yours.

This is for kitchen and bath shops already doing great work and just losing the online fight.

If that’s you, it’s the most useful half hour you’ll spend on your business this month.

Book your 30-minute strategy session →

The questions a burned owner actually asks.

What exactly do you do?

Two things.

I get you found by the homeowners already searching for your work. And I make you the one they pick when they look you up.

That means rebuilding your Google so it shows the shop you actually are, and building a website that turns a curious click into a booked estimate.

Then I keep it working every month. That’s it.

Aren’t you just another marketer who’ll rip me off?

Fair question. You’ve earned it.

Here’s the difference. I don’t sell leads. I don’t promise rankings. And I don’t lock you in.

I show you the plan before you pay me a dollar. You see the work every week. You own everything I build.

The last guy mailed you charts while your phone stayed quiet. I’d rather you judge me on homeowners calling.

Do you promise me leads or rankings?

No, and run from anyone who does.

“Leads” is the Angi game, the same homeowner sold to four shops at once. Rankings nobody controls, not me, not the guy who swore he did.

What I promise is the work, the deadline, and more homeowners finding you and calling than do today. Beat that number in 90 days, or you stop paying and I keep going.

That’s a promise I can actually keep.

Do I own my website and Google profile?

All of it. Always.

The site, the domain, the Google profile, the photos, the content. It’s yours from day one.

Most agencies hold it hostage so you can’t leave. Fire me any month and it all walks out the door with you.

I’d rather earn next month than trap you in it.

How much does it cost?

Depends what you need, and you’ll know the number before you commit to anything.

The website is a flat quote, $3,500 to $6,000, and you see the homepage before you pay for the build. Ongoing Google work runs $997 a month, month to month.

One kitchen is $20,000 to $30,000 to you. Win back a single homeowner you’d have lost, and it’s already paid for itself.

We’ll talk real numbers on the call.

I’m slammed on jobs. How much of my time does this take?

Almost none. That’s the point.

I need an hour up front to learn your business and your work. After that, I do the building.

The only ongoing thing I ask is that you ask happy customers for a review, and that you call back the homeowners who reach out. You’re doing that part anyway.

You stay on the tools. I handle the screen.

Why should I trust you over a bigger agency?

Because a bigger agency has a hundred clients, and you’d be a line item.

I only work with kitchen and bath shops, and I take a handful at a time. I designed your homepage myself. I know your numbers by name.

The big shop sells you the same playbook they sell a dentist and a roofer. I know why a homeowner picks one remodeler over another, because it’s the only thing I do.

You’re not a ticket in a queue. You’re the work.

You’ll walk away with the exact plan to beat them.

30 minutes, and you leave knowing precisely what to fix, in what order, to start winning the jobs going to the guy down the road. Hire me to run it or run it yourself. Kitchen and bath only.

Book your 30-minute strategy session →
97% read reviews before they pick a local business
88% of mobile local searches end in a call or visit that day
45% now use AI to find local pros, up from 6% a year ago
5–9% more revenue from a single extra star
